hypothesis: Incorporating three-dimensional conformational information—specifically low-energy conformer descriptors encoded as structured text sequences (e.g., serialized distance matrices or torsion angle strings)—as an eighth modality within the STR-Bamba unified vocabulary will create a distinct, separable cluster in the multimodal latent space that improves property prediction performance on quantum mechanical tasks (QM9, QM8) beyond current state-of-the-art results, because the existing seven textual representations lack explicit 3D geometric encoding and the Mamba-2 recurrent mechanism can capture long-range positional dependencies in conformer sequences that attention-based models cannot efficiently process at equivalent context lengths.
